title: Campomanesia recurvata
intro: Campomanesia recurvata, also known as the Brazilian guava or Brazilian cherry, is a small shrub or tree native to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, belonging to the Myrtaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Campomanesia recurvata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical applications for skin and wound care.
